channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Success Lab: Habits for Success
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - There is one, important factor that can significantly impact your physical health and your cognitive abilities. It is the foundation upon which learning and recovery are built. Come and learn about what you can do to improve your overall success through positive habit building and find out what that one thing is!
item.author: Students

item.title: Leaders in Action: Conversation with Captain David J. Latta ’09 Scheduled for March 31
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM - Join host Prof. Azita Hirsa as she welcomes Captain David J. Latta ’09G for a Leaders in Action conversation, Leadership in Action: Ethics from a Career in the Nuclear Navy. Drawing from his experience in the U.S. Nuclear Navy, Captain Latta will share insights on leadership, accountability, and ethical decision-making in high-stakes environments.
item.author: Community

item.title: Lake Sediment Cores, Finnish Exceptionalism and More
item.description: A snapshot of some of the latest research from RPI experts.
item.author: Community

item.title: The Center for Architecture Science and Ecology Announces $25K Seed Grant
item.description: CASE is an institute wide research center designing, developing and translating next generation technology for a healthy and resilient built environment. This $25,000 grant will directly support a 1 year research project to address the research goals designated by CASE: Climate Adaptation, Circular Economies, Environmental Intelligence and Planetary Health. The grant is designed to foster interdisciplinary and cross-institution collaboration and enhance competitiveness for external funding.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Athletics Celebrates 50 Years of Women’s Sports
item.description: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute proudly celebrates 50 years of women’s varsity athletics—a milestone that reflects decades of progress, achievement, and opportunity for generations of student-athletes. What began as a commitment to expanding access in intercollegiate athletics has grown into a vibrant and competitive tradition that continues to shape the RPI community today.
item.author: Community

item.title: Side by Side Concert 2026
item.description: Tuesday / March 31, 2026 at 7:30 pm The combined forces of the RPI Orchestra and Empire State Youth Orchestra’s Symphony Orchestra unite to perform Gustav Mahler’s epic Symphony no. 1. The program will be led by RPI music faculty Dr. Robert Whalen, and will also include a performance of Edvard Grieg’s famous Concerto for Piano and Orchestra by 2025 Concerto Competition winner Armstrong Wang ’27. Presented by School of HASS Performance Rensselaer Orchestra and ESYO
item.author: Community

item.title: RARE High School Student Advances to International Science and Engineering Fair
item.description: The Rensselaer Astrobiology Research and Education (RARE) Center congratulates a local high school student, Mikaila, on being named a finalist for the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) in May 2026. After earning first place at the Westchester Science & Engineering Fair and recognition from the Association for Women Geoscientists, she will present research conducted over two summers at the RARE Center.
item.author: Community

item.title: Celebrate the Future Generations of Game Designers at GameFest 2026
item.description: GameFest returns to EMPAC on Saturday, March 28 from 12pm-7pm. GameFest is an annual showcase of student talent in game development, bringing together students from around New York State and the Northeast to exhibit their games. This year’s event will feature talks and film screenings from visiting artist, competitive eSports matches, and a retro gaming lounge.
item.author: Community

item.title: Fair Game Films: A Selected Screening of the Films of Ericka Beckman
item.description: As a part of the Department of Games and Experiential Media’s “Gamefest” and Arts’ iEar Presents, this screening and discussion will draw on Beckman’s longstanding exploration of how physical action, and our memory of it, constructs cultural images and influences both our behavior and perception of reality. Visit EMPAC Theater at 2:15pm on March 28.
item.author: Community

item.title: Commencement Volunteer Opportunities
item.description:
Commencement is one of the best opportunities at RPI to connect with fellow faculty, staff, and our graduating students! To ensure a memorable experience for our graduating class, we need assistance from everyone. Please consider volunteering at commencement or finding other ways to support the event leading up to the big day. Please click the title to access the online form and confirm your participation with a manager beforehand.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
